intent: After input extension handlers run, rebuild agent.state.messages from the session manager before constructing the next prompt. This lets pi-sync reopen/branch the session during input handling so the model request uses the refreshed lane head without injecting hidden context.
verify: // Refresh agent transcript in case input hooks changed the active session leaf.

references.reason: pi-sync input hooks refresh/branch session state to the lane head before forwarding prompts to the host; Pi must rebuild agent state before prompt construction so the model sees normal session history without hidden lane metadata.

intent: Expose a no-op ExtensionUIContext.replayAgentEvent in non-interactive modes so pi-sync can call one UI API regardless of mode.

references.reason: pi-sync needs a native UI replay hook so attached Pi terminals can render the same event stream instead of approximating it in a widget.

intent: Expose ExtensionUIContext.replayAgentEvent in interactive mode by routing through InteractiveMode.handleEvent, and optionally through local extension handlers, giving synced terminals native Pi rendering plus extension-owned UI side effects for mirrored agent/session events.

references.reason: Allows remote same-session events to be consumed by Pi's normal interactive renderer and local UI extensions, making exact mirrored UI possible without ad hoc widgets.

intent: Type the ExtensionUIContext.replayAgentEvent hook used by pi-sync. It accepts raw AgentSession event-shaped objects and replays them through the UI renderer when available, optionally emitting local extension handlers for extension-owned UI.
verify: replayAgentEvent(event: unknown, options?: { emitExtensions?: boolean }): Promise<void>;

references.reason: Provides TypeScript surface for the native UI replay hook and extension replay option.

intent: Render non-persistent live tree markers from PI_SYNC_TREE_MARKERS before the active-path dot so synced terminals can show where live pi-sync lanes are pointed without writing labels into the session.

references.reason: pi-sync exports ephemeral live lane-head markers for the tree selector; this keeps lane position debugging visible while avoiding persistent label entries or sync-specific transcript UI.

intent: Place the live pi-sync lane marker between the cursor/prefix and active-path dot in each session-tree row.

references.reason: Shows live sync lane labels at their current tree heads, e.g. `› 2 • assistant`, so users can see where other attached terminals are pointed.

intent: Render the live pi-sync lane marker directly after the selector cursor, before tree indentation, so nested tree rows still show lane position next to `›`.
find: let line = cursor + theme.fg("dim", prefix) + foldMarker + liveMarker + pathMarker + label + labelTimestamp + content;
replace: let line = cursor + liveMarker + theme.fg("dim", prefix) + foldMarker + pathMarker + label + labelTimestamp + content;
verify: let line = cursor + liveMarker + theme.fg("dim", prefix) + foldMarker + pathMarker + label + labelTimestamp + content;

references.reason: Keeps live lane markers visible beside the tree cursor even for deeply nested branch rows.
